description:
Advances: none (epic wrapper — children advance specled.spec_review)

Follow-up to specled_-qm1. The mix spec.review HTML report has a working layout and spec-first navigation, but the diagram + sync-checklist leg classifier in lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex (lines 278-283, 423-489) is wired to a spec-validate / co-change vocabulary, not the triangle vocabulary documented in docs/concepts.md.

Result: branch_guard_realization_drift, branch_guard_dangling_binding, branch_guard_untested_realization, branch_guard_untethered_test, branch_guard_underspecified_realization, overlap/*, append_only/*, and detector_unavailable currently land only in the flat "All findings" list (with a subject badge). The diagram can read "in sync" while a triangle-stressing PR is sideways — the eye trusts the diagram first.

This epic re-aligns the report's classification engine with the triangle described in docs/concepts.md, fills first-class gaps (detector_unavailable, no-realized_by advisory), and trims redundancy. v1 (specled_-qm1) was layout polish; v2 is conceptual alignment.

Children are ranked. #1 and #2 are load-bearing — the rest assume a triangle-aware classifier exists.

Reference analysis: conversation with the user 2026-05-04 walking the rendered _build/spec_review.html against the branch-guard scenario list.

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR NOTE (2026-05-04)

Worktree: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-qm1
Epic-branch: specled_-qm1/spec-review-html

All child stage worktrees branch off this epic-branch. Stages are merged back into
the epic by the orchestrator (not by /implement) to coordinate parallel waves.

title: [EPIC] spec.review v2: triangle-aligned classification
